skill.name: website-to-video
skill.description: Turn a captured website into a HyperFrames video using screenshots, brand assets, storyboard planning, narration, captions, and validation checks.
skill.summary: Build a website showcase video from a URL with capture, storyboard, voice, motion, and preview delivery.
skill.author: heygen-com

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, filesystem, and credential patterns. Review found many markdown and regex false positives, but confirmed that the skill intentionally captures websites, handles API keys, calls third-party services, writes project artifacts, and runs local CLI commands. No prompt injection or confirmed malicious intent was found, so this is high risk rather than blocked.

high_findings.title: Credential Handling Guidance for External Services
high_findings.description: The skill asks users to place Gemini, ElevenLabs, and HeyGen API keys in .env files or paste keys into chat. This is functional for TTS and image descriptions, but it creates credential exposure risk in shared workspaces and transcripts.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines explicitly discuss API keys, .env files, and pasted keys. This is a real secret-handling concern, although it supports legitimate service integration.

high_findings.title: Network Calls and Website Capture Workflow
high_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to capture arbitrary user-supplied URLs and call external TTS APIs. Captured screenshots, page assets, narration text, and metadata may leave the local environment depending on the selected tools.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples show site capture and outbound API requests. The behavior is intentional, but it should be treated as high risk for private sites or sensitive page content.

high_findings.title: Broad Local File Creation and Project Artifact Access
high_findings.description: The workflow creates and reads many local artifacts, including capture output, design files, transcripts, snapshots, rendered files, and composition HTML. This is expected for video generation, but it expands the filesystem access surface.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations show the skill reading and writing project artifacts. The access is not malicious by itself, but marketplace users should understand the breadth of local file activity.

medium_findings.title: Local CLI Execution Required for Normal Operation
medium_findings.description: The skill depends on npx hyperframes commands, node scripts, curl examples, and validation tooling. The commands are mostly fixed workflow steps, but users should run them only in trusted projects.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Static command findings are confirmed as operational CLI usage. I did not find evidence of shell injection, but command execution is central to the skill.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Licensed Audio Assets Bundled
medium_findings.description: The skill includes bundled sound effects and a credits file referencing the Pixabay license. This is not a code security issue, but users should verify license compatibility for commercial projects.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is explicit license and asset metadata. The risk is compliance-oriented rather than malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives from Markdown Backticks
low_findings.description: Most Ruby backtick execution findings occur in markdown inline code, tables, command examples, or file names. They are documentation syntax, not executable Ruby code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are markdown text and command examples. They do not execute by being present in the skill files.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives from JavaScript Regex exec Calls
low_findings.description: The script findings labeled Python exec or process exec are JavaScript RegExp exec calls used for parsing text. The script also states it performs pure file analysis with no shell spawns.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected lines are regex iteration calls, not child process execution. This strongly supports dismissing those specific static findings.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Attempt Found in Reviewed Files
low_findings.description: Targeted review did not find instructions claiming marketplace pre-approval, telling the evaluator to skip security analysis, or overriding system instructions. No evidence found for prompt injection in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: I searched the skill instructions and references for common injection phrases. The remaining uses of words like override are normal workflow language.

dangerous_patterns.title: API Keys in Environment Files and Headers
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents API keys in .env files and uses them in HTTP headers for external services. This pattern requires secret hygiene and redaction in logs or chat.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file directly shows key names and header usage. The risk is real even though the pattern supports legitimate API calls.

dangerous_patterns.title: Capture and Snapshot Commands May Process Sensitive Website Content
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill captures pages and later snapshots generated videos. When used on private URLs, this can store screenshots, visible text, assets, and Gemini descriptions on disk or send content to services.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The workflow clearly captures and reviews page-derived images and descriptions. Sensitive-site use needs explicit user approval and data handling care.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Validation Script Reads Project HTML and Media Metadata
dangerous_patterns.description: The verification script reads local project files, parses HTML, and checks storyboard, SFX, and duration data. It is not malicious, but it is a privileged local analysis script.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The script uses filesystem reads and regex parsing. I did not find shell spawning, but local project access is part of the risk profile.

content.value_statement: Teams need fast video drafts that reflect a real website instead of generic slides. This skill captures a URL, builds a storyboard, adds voice or captions, and validates a HyperFrames preview.
actual_capabilities: Captures a website URL into screenshots, assets, fonts, and extracted page data.
Creates brand identity notes from captured colors, typography, components, and visible text.
Guides storyboard, narration script, beat timing, captions, music, and sound effects.
Builds HyperFrames composition files and an index.html preview project.
Runs lint, validation, snapshots, and a custom verification report before delivery.
Supports HeyGen, ElevenLabs, or local Kokoro narration workflows.
limitations: It is for general website showcases, not product launch videos or topic explainers.
Private websites may expose screenshots, text, and assets during capture or review.
External TTS and Gemini features require user-managed API keys and service access.
It delivers a preview by default and renders MP4 only when the user asks.

use_cases.title: Portfolio Site Reel
use_cases.description: Turn a portfolio URL into a short branded reel for proposals, social posts, or client review.

use_cases.title: Homepage Tour
use_cases.description: Create a narrated walkthrough that explains a website message, structure, visuals, and calls to action.

use_cases.title: Content Site Teaser
use_cases.description: Convert a blog, community page, or landing page into a concise video preview for distribution.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Site Clip
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a short social video
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 15-second social clip from this website: [URL]. Use the site visuals and keep the message simple.

prompt_templates.title: Narrated Site Tour
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request with audience and format
prompt_templates.prompt: Make a 30-second narrated website tour for [audience]. Use [landscape/portrait/square] format and focus on [main message].

prompt_templates.title: Brand-Led Showcase
prompt_templates.prompt: Capture [URL] and create a polished showcase video. Match the brand colors, use captured assets, include captions, and avoid generic stock visuals.

prompt_templates.title: Autonomous Draft With Review Gates
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a complete HyperFrames preview from [URL]. Decide the creative direction, show required gates, and disclose every verification result before delivery.

output_examples.input: A founder asks for a 20-second video from a new landing page.
output_examples.output: The skill captures the page, drafts a five-beat storyboard, writes short narration, builds motion scenes, and delivers a Studio preview URL.

output_examples.input: A designer wants a silent square reel from a portfolio site.
output: A brand summary based on the captured site.
A concise storyboard with visual beats.
A captioned HyperFrames preview using site assets.

output_examples.input: A team asks for final validation before sharing a website video.
output_examples.output: The skill runs lint, validate, snapshots, audio checks, and the custom verification script, then reports what passed and what needs review.

best_practices: Use public or approved URLs, and avoid capturing private dashboards unless the user confirms consent.
Ask for the core message and audience before choosing visuals or narration style.
Paste real validation evidence in the final summary, including any skipped checks.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for product launch promos when the product-launch-video skill is a better route.
Do not paste API keys into shared chats or commit .env files to source control.
Do not claim the video is ready if snapshots, audio, or validation checks were skipped.

faq.question: Does this skill render the final MP4 automatically?
faq.answer: No. It delivers a Studio preview first and renders only after the user explicitly asks.

faq.question: Can it work without paid APIs?
faq.answer: Yes. Base capture works without API keys, and Kokoro can generate local draft narration.

faq.question: What data does it capture from a site?
faq.answer: It can capture screenshots, assets, fonts, visible text, design tokens, and page-derived metadata.

faq.question: Is it safe for private websites?
faq.answer: Use caution. Private pages may expose screenshots, text, and assets to local files or external review tools.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What should users review before publishing?
faq.answer: Review captured assets, script accuracy, brand fit, captions, audio timing, licenses, and the verification report.
